entries.name: Example Selective Admission Gate
entries.summary: Example-only proposed mechanism that assigns an admission decision to a candidate event before persistent storage. No effectiveness claim or empirical result is recorded.

hypotheses.statement: A provenance-aware admission policy may reduce later retrieval of deliberately irrelevant events while preserving source-sensitive recall under declared test conditions.
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: On preregistered held-out synthetic scenarios, the candidate policy must improve irrelevant-event retrieval rate relative to the store-all baseline without exceeding the declared non-inferiority margin for source-sensitive recall; otherwise the hypothesis is not supported.
hypotheses.null_hypothesis: The candidate policy does not satisfy both the irrelevant-event and source-sensitive-recall criteria relative to the store-all baseline.
assumptions: Scenario relevance labels are specified before evaluation.
Candidate and baseline receive identical event streams and retrieval requests.

inputs: Candidate event, provenance, scope, timestamp, and declared policy context
outputs: Admission decision, reason code, confidence representation, and policy version
state_changes: An accepted event becomes eligible for encoding; rejection does not create persistent memory state
failure_signals: Malformed provenance, unavailable policy, ambiguous authority, or invalid scope
replaceable_components: Policy evaluator, storage adapter, feature extractor, and confidence estimator

declared_architectural_assumptions: Events expose stable provenance and scope fields.
Policy decisions can be logged without storing rejected event content.

rejection_criteria: Reject the hypothesis if either preregistered joint success condition fails on the held-out suite.
Invalidate a run if candidate and baseline do not receive byte-identical scenario inputs.

security_and_privacy_analysis.threat_model_scope: Example scope: admission inputs, policy configuration, decision logs, and downstream storage eligibility.
trust_boundaries: Untrusted event source to admission policy
Admission policy to persistent storage adapter
adversary_capabilities: Submit crafted event content and forged in-band instructions
Repeat candidate events to attempt policy evasion

allowed: Observe admission or rejection for adversary-submitted events
denied: Read other users' event content or policy state
assumptions: The test harness enforces user-scope isolation outside the mechanism under test.

in_scope: Decision reason codes and response timing
leakage_assumptions: Logs are visible only to principals authorized for the same user scope.

security_and_privacy_analysis.adversary_success_criterion: Cause unauthorized persistent admission, infer protected policy or event information, or affect another user's memory scope.
authority_boundaries: The mechanism proposes an admission outcome but cannot expand user scope or override deletion authority.
identified_risks: Persistent prompt injection through admitted content
Sensitive-data retention caused by an erroneous admission
Cross-user leakage caused by incorrect scope binding
planned_controls: Typed scope checks, provenance validation, reason-coded policy decisions, and append-only decision audit records
residual_risks: Semantically deceptive content may evade a policy despite valid provenance.
deletion_requirements: Rejected content must not be copied into persistent diagnostic logs.
Accepted records must retain a deletion handle tied to user scope.

limitations.description: The initial scenarios are synthetic and cannot establish performance on natural longitudinal conversations.
limitations.risk_if_ignored: A benchmark-specific policy could be mistaken for a generally effective admission mechanism.
limitations.next_check: Define a separately governed, privacy-reviewed naturalistic evaluation after synthetic protocol validation.
